comes to researching content for your page. And this also applies for your ads as well. There are a few different ways you can do this. There's so many content research tools out there. I'll just give you an example of four ways that I will do it to give me different ideas and angles. Now, this is a tool called Ubersuggest, which is owned by Neil Patel. You can type in keywords and get content ideas back. So I've typed in Mother's Day here and I've got some ideas back for kind of blogs. So I can click into these blogs and get an idea of what I could write about it. So if I'm doing a Mother's Day campaign and I want to share something on my page related to Mother's Day, I can look not only at the content, but also the type of engagement that the posts have got on these two different platforms. So you can have a look here at the engagement across these different platforms. These are kind of blog posts that have come up to give me an idea of kind of what's available. This one you do need to pay to get a bit more. Whereas Uber suggests you can get away with not paying. And with SEMrush, this is now going right back to my days into paid search. And I used to do this a lot for paid search and organic searches, do keyword research on tools like SEMrush. And what it allows you to do here is not just kind of look at the kind of search terms as you normally would, like what keywords people are searching, but you can also see what kind of results are coming up in the search pages as well and scan across different types of keywords as well. So for example, if I typed, as I've typed in Mother's Day here and I'm looking at the states, I can see the keyword variations as well and what kind of questions people are asking. So for example, when is Mother's Day, for example, if let's say four weeks ahead of your Mother's Day campaign, you posted on your page to say, when is Mother's Day is a question and you write a post, maybe have some images, etc. and just expand a bit about Mother's Day, you're priming your content around that, for example. And it just gives you ideas of kind of what people are thinking about ahead of Mother's Day and how you can build that into your content strategy for your ads and also your page as well.
